verdict.answer_first: Tie leaning B: B opens with the concrete cause, "u.profile is undefined," while A also answers immediately with "the undefined thing is u.profile."
verdict.decisiveness: B commits more because it gives an actual fix path with code: "if (!u) throw" and "return u.profile?.name ?? null"; A stops at "depends on what the caller expects."
verdict.fabrication: Both speculate on causes; B invents more examples with "optional relation, not-yet-completed signup, soft-deleted," while A's "separate collection" is also not given.
verdict.actionability: B is easier to act on because it gives guarded code and a confirmation step: "log the offending record."
